member.name: Sir Bernard Jenkin
member.role: MP for Harwich and North Essex
member.synopsis: Sir Bernard Jenkin is the Conservative MP for Harwich and North Essex , and has been an MP continually since 9 April 1992.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will set out in detail where he expects to be able to fulfill the commitment of £10.7bn included in the spending projections on page 73 of the Defence Investment Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defence is committed to efficiency and ensuring every pound spent on Defence is used in the best way for our Armed Forces and the taxpayer. Section 4 of the Defence Investment Plan (DIP), published 30 June 2026, outlines our extensive Reform and Efficienc...

writtenQuestions.heading: Livestock: Exports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if she will consider allowing the export of live animals to the EU as part of the dynamic alignment provisions of the proposed sanitary and phytosanitary agreement with the EU.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not currently prohibit the export of live animals to the EU, except for slaughter and fattening. This Government is currently negotiating a Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) Agreement to make agrifood trade with our biggest market cheap...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: UK Membership of EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment has been made by her Department of the statistical reliability of the OBR's claim that the UK economy is growing more slowly than if the UK had remained in the EU after 2021; and if she will set out
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) is the Government’s independent official forecaster and is responsible for producing economic and fiscal forecasts.The OBR has included assessments of the economic impacts of leaving the EU in its forecasts since...

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: EU Law
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of measures proposed in the EU reset on economic growth, taking account costs on business and the taxpayer of, inter allia, i) new regulations and dire
writtenQuestions.answerText: At a time of great global uncertainty, we must deepen our relationships with allies whose values we share and whose interests are tied to our own. This is why we will pursue a closer relationship with the EU where it is in our national interest to do so. ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Growth: Brexit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the impact of leaving the EU on the underlying growth rate of the UK economy; and what recent analysis her Department has undertaken on that issue.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) is the government’s official forecaster. The OBR has included assessments of the economic impacts of leaving the EU in its forecasts since 2016. In March 2020 the OBR estimated that, as a result of the deal negot...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Appointments: Vetting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many times the assessments made by UKSV arising from the developed vetting of individuals proposed for public appointments since the enactment of the Constitutional Reform and Governance Act 2010 come into f
writtenQuestions.answerText: UKSV does not centrally categorise individuals by their specific terms of appointment, such as whether they are a public appointee. In line with the practice followed by successive administrations, the Government does not otherwise comment on security mat...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Suffolk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate he has made of the proportion of UK electricity that will pass through the Bramford substation (a) in an average year and (b) at periods of highest demand through Bramford after
writtenQuestions.answerText: This information is not held centrally.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she will give a substantive reply to the letter from the hon. Member for Harwich and North Essex dated 18 November 2025 on the road/rail crossing at Manningtree.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A response was sent on 12th March.

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Essex
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, pursuant to his decision for postpone the Mayoral election in Essex, if he will make an estimate of the cost to local authorities of the preparations for that election, including
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made by central government. Spend on these elections is a matter for the relevant local authorities.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Regulatory Review
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, which Ministers are responsible for overseeing the implementation of the recommendations of the Nuclear Regulatory Review 2025; and if he will set out the timeline for their implementation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The recommendations of the Taskforce will radically move the dial on the regulatory regime for civil and defence nuclear in this country, and ministers from a number of government departments will have a role in overseeing the implementation of the recomm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: A1331: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what recent progress he has made with Cabinet colleagues on allocating funding for the proposed A1331 link road.
writtenQuestions.answerText: £100m from the Housing Infrastructure Fund has been provided to Essex County Council to deliver Phase 1 of the A1331 Link Road, which is under construction. We have been working closely with the council and are expecting them to submit a formal request to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office and Civil Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if the Government will investigate the source of internal Government briefings on the future of the Cabinet Secretary and Head of the Civil Service; and if the Prime Minister will make a statement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Secretary continues to have the support of the Prime Minister. They are working closely together to deliver on the priorities of the British public.

writtenQuestions.heading: Statutory Instruments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Leader of the House, if she will publish a list of the statutory instruments made since the last general election and set out the provisions in the parent act of Parliament which are relied upon to provide statutory authority for the making of
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested can be found at https://www.legislation.gov.uk/uksi.

writtenQuestions.heading: Liverpool Street Station
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the redevelopment of Liverpool Street Station on the surrounding heritage buildings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The initial plans brought forward by a previous developer have now been replaced by a revised scheme. This new approach seeks to address heritage concerns while preserving the benefits of commercial development. Crucially, it enables significant improveme...

writtenQuestions.heading: Liverpool Street Station: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the redevelopment of Liverpool Street Station on the economy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The redevelopment of Liverpool Street Station will enable £500m investment in the busiest station in the UK at no cost to the taxpayer. Officials in my Department are engaged with Network Rail Property and Planning teams around the redevelopment proposal...

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Government: Reorganisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if she will make an assessment of the cost to the public purse of local government reorganisation in Essex.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The overall case for local government reorganisation is set out the English Devolution White Paper.A PWC 2020 report estimated that reorganisation of the then 25 two-tier areas to a single unitary would have a one-off cost of £400 million, with the potent...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office and Prime Minister: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many (a) officials and (b) advisers are employed by (i) his Department and (ii) Number 10 Downing Street.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the number of officials in the Cabinet Office is published monthly at https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/cabinet-office-workforce-management-data-2023-to-2024 and Business Unit level data including for Business Units in Number 10 Downing S...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Diagnostic Centres: Essex and Suffolk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to his Oral Statement of 6 January 2026 on Health and Adult Social Care Reform, Official Report, Column 597, where new diagnostic centres in (a) Essex and (b) Suffolk will be based.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Elective Reform Plan, published on 6 January 2025, sets out that we will deliver additional community diagnostic centre (CDC) capacity in 2025/26 by expanding a number of existing CDCs and building up to five new ones. Capital investment agreed at the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: National Grid: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the (a) level of skilled labour and (b) extent of the shortage of the particular skills required to deliver the proposed upgrades to the National Grid electrici
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reinforcing Great Britain’s electricity network to meet net zero will require a range of skills in engineering, IT professionals, physical scientist and environmental professionals, project managers, chartered surveyors and quality control, metal working ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Unitary Councils

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Case For Local Government Reorganisation in the English Devolution White Paper, states “In 2020 a PwC report, “Evaluating the importance of scale in proposals for local government reorganisation”, for the County Councils Network, estimated that reorga...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

items.text: Role, work or services: Speaking engagement Until: 15 April 2026. Payer: El Espaniol, (National newspaper in Spain), Avenida de Burgos 16, 28036 Comunidad de Madrid, Spain (Registered 6 May 2026)

Payment: £1,264.76 This fee was in addition to the expenses covered for the visit to Madrid which is separately declared. Received on: 29 April 2026. Hours: 4 hrs The engagement involved attending the Wake Up Spain defence conference in Madrid, as well as participating in one of the sessions, and at a lunch discussion, to discuss a CIVITAS paper I co-authored. (Registered 6 May 2026)

Role, work or services: Writing articles Until: 28 May 2026. Payer: Mace Publishing Limited Company number 15121315 (58190 - Other publishing activities), Wsm Connect House 133-137 Alexandra Road, Wimbledon SW19 7JY (Registered 3 June 2026)
Payment: £300 Received on: 28 May 2026. Hours: 4 hrs Approx. (Registered 3 June 2026)

A Vice President of Combat Stress, a charity offering mental health services to veterans. This is an unpaid role. (Registered 15 December 2021)
Director and Chairman of Beach Road Water Ltd, a non-profit company limited by guarantee. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 20 July 2011 (Registered 11 December 2023)

I am a Trustee of the Parliament Choir, which was an All Party Parliamentary Group until 2019, but is now an autonomous body under the patronage of the Speaker of the House of Commons and the Lords Speaker. The choir is open to all who work in Parliament. Date interest arose: 1 January 2010 (Registered 21 October 2025)
